Provider Dashboard — Brooke Welsh, WHNP

AI Protocol Builder

Draft data-backed protocols from consult recordings, notes, and cohort outcomes

156 Active Patients
5 Cohorts
AI-assisted draft

From consult recording to draft protocol in < 60 seconds.

Upload (or auto-import) a consult recording and prior notes. The system transcribes, matches the patient against cohorts of similar patients you've already treated, and drafts a starting protocol with rationale and source citations. Brooke and Nicki review and approve — nothing reaches the patient without sign-off.

1 Capture
2 Transcribe + extract
3 Cohort match
4 Draft protocol
5 Provider review

Inputs (auto-collected)

Consult recording — 38 min
Uploaded Apr 11, 2026 · transcribed via HIPAA-compliant service
Prior F/U notes (3 visits)
Pulled from existing EMR via secure import
Latest lab panel
Access Medical Labs · 7 markers parsed
Body comp + vitals
DEXA from Apr 11, 2026

Patient snapshot

Jennifer Smith · 42 · ID P002

Presenting: Worsening fatigue, sleep disruption, 8lb weight gain over 6mo, cycle irregularity

estradiol
52 pg/mL (fluctuating)
progesterone
1.8 ng/mL (low for luteal)
fsh
18 mIU/mL (elevated)
tsh
2.4 (normal)
freeTestosterone
1.2 ng/dL (low-normal)
a1c
5.5%
vitaminD
28 ng/mL (low)
LT
Best-match cohort
Low Testosterone (Male)
47 similar patients · top protocol score 94/100

Men 35-65 presenting with fatigue, low libido, body comp changes. Total T <400 ng/dL at intake.

View full cohort →

Draft protocol

AI draft · awaiting provider review
Working diagnosis

Perimenopausal symptom cluster with low progesterone and Vitamin D insufficiency. Differential: subclinical insulin resistance given borderline A1C.

Recommended plan
Hormone
Estradiol pellet 75mg + oral micronized progesterone 100mg PM (cyclic days 14-28)
↳ Matches top-effectiveness protocol from 38 perimenopause patients with symptom score -68%
Supplement
Vitamin D3/K2 5000 IU AM (target 50-80 ng/mL)
↳ Low at 28; correlates with sleep + mood improvements
Supplement
Magnesium glycinate 400mg PM
↳ Sleep support, common in perimenopause cohort
Supplement
Omega-3 2g/day
↳ Cardio-metabolic protective, mild mood benefit
Lifestyle
Strength training 3x/wk + protein 100g/day
↳ Lean mass preservation (cohort avg -1.2 lbs LBM if not addressed)
Monitoring
Recheck full panel + A1C at 12 weeks
↳ Standard cohort follow-up cadence
Expected outcome

Based on 38-patient cohort: symptom score -68% by week 16, sleep +3.6/5, weight stabilization or -8 to -12 lbs.

Watch-fors

If A1C trends upward at 12wk recheck, consider adding metabolic cohort protocol overlay (low-dose tirzepatide).

Sources cited
  • F/U notes from prior consult (2026-03-20) -- patient mentioned worsening sleep
  • Cohort: Perimenopause (n=38, top-effectiveness protocol, score 91)
  • Lab trend: rising FSH consistent with late-stage perimenopause
  • Brooke's prior protocol for similar patient (Sarah Williams, P004) -- modified for lower BMI

Provider must review and approve. Draft is generated from de-identified pattern matching; no protocol reaches the patient without provider sign-off.
